/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:3:**Paper:** ESG Preference, Institutional Trading, and Stock Return Patterns
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:6:**Source File:** /Users/my/output/ESG Preference.docx
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:11:How do ESG preferences and attention constraints of socially responsible (SR) institutions shape their trading behavior and the return patterns of the stocks they favor? The paper asks whether ESG-driven demand alters price discovery and generates predictable return spreads.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:16:Provides the first empirical mapping from ESG preference to institutional trading/returns by linking (i) a novel SR institutional ownership measure built from MSCI ESG KLD holdings to (ii) mispricing signals (SUE/SYY) and (iii) subsequent portfolio returns. The work highlights how ESG-driven taste-based demand creates a persistent predictability channel.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:21:No formal theoretical model; the authors motivate the analysis with a limited-attention mechanism in which SR institutions focus on ESG-salient stocks, restricting the set of trading signals they process and slowing price discovery outside that set.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:26:CRSP stock returns/prices/volumes (January 1996–December 2016) merged with MSCI ESG KLD Stats ESG scores. Key constructs include SR_IO (weight-share of SR institutions), SUE score (standardized unexpected earnings), and SYY score (composite mispricing signal). Supplementary data cover analyst coverage, liquidity, short-sale variables, and firm characteristics.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:31:[Omitted long matching line]
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:36:- SR instituions adjust ownership less to SUE/SYY signals, indicating they trade within a narrower ESG-preferred universe (Table 2).
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:40:- Triple sorts and size interactions reveal ESG preference predicts returns even when controlling for ESG scores and firm size (Tables 11–12).
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:45:- Repeat analyses in 1996–2003 sample to show the ESG effect emerges after the ESG investing boom.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:46:- Alternative SR definitions (Asset4 ESG) and ESG score splits (Figure 3).
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:49:- Triple sorts incorporate ESG score and size to rule out confounding firm characteristics (Tables 11–12).
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:54:- SR classification hinges on ESG score availability and the chosen cutoff, which may evolve as ESG coverage expands.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:61:Builds on ESG/normative demand literature (e.g., Hong & Kacperczyk 2009, Hartzmark & Sussman 2019) and the limits-to-arbitrage/mispricing literature (Chordia et al. 2014, Hou & Moskowitz 2005). Extends investment constraint narratives by tracing how ESG taste-based demand alters trading and pricing specifically through institutional holdings and mispricing signals.
/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md:66:Serves as the structured source material for the ESG Preference slide deck: it supplies the sketch for motivation, data/measures, trading results, return patterns, and robustness figures that we thread throughout the lecture. The digest ensures downstream skills (create-slides, econometric-review) share a consistent understanding of the paper's contributions.

content.text:
Summary of the repository exploration (read-only, per instructions):

1. **ESG literature digest** – `/Users/my/quality_reports/paper_digest_ESG_Preference.md` is a structured summary of the “ESG Preference, Institutional Trading, and Stock Return Patterns” paper, including motivation, data, core findings, robustness checks, and how it feeds downstream slides/skills. This is the most relevant existing literature-review-style asset for ESG ratings.

2. **Plan capturing prior ESG effort** – `/Users/my/quality_reports/plans/2026-03-24_esg-preference-slides.md` documents the ESG preference slides verification attempt (Quarto rendering note). Useful for understanding what work has already been scoped around that paper.

3. **Other searches** – No files under `quality_reports/`, `papers/`, or the current workspace explicitly mention “local newspaper,” “local newspaper closures,” “newsroom,” or a similar topic, so there appear to be no literature-review artifacts on newspaper closures in this repo. The grep/scan also found no relevant matches outside of the Trash directory (which mostly contains workflow docs unrelated to the requested topic).

Let me know if you want me to dig into other directories (e.g., `/Users/my/output/`), look up more ESG literature sources, or if you’d like help drafting a focused literature review plan once you clarify the target topic.
